tasks.title: Scaffold runnable app foundation
tasks.objective: Choose a pragmatic web app stack for this empty product repo and create the minimal runnable application, lint/test scripts, and README setup notes.
authority: May choose the initial product stack because no stack is specified in the repo
Keep the scaffold minimal and runnable
Do not implement full product flows in this task
acceptance_criteria: A local development app can start successfully
Baseline lint/type/test or equivalent checks are configured
README.md documents install, run, and verification commands
The app has a clear source directory structure for domain, persistence, and UI work
verification: Run the documented baseline verification command
Start the app locally or run a framework-provided smoke check
tasks.stop_condition: Stop once the foundation is runnable and ready for domain work.
tasks.evidence: 2026-05-30 00:13 EDT: Added dependency-free Node.js app scaffold with src/domain, src/persistence, src/server, src/ui, scripts/lint.mjs, tests/foundation.test.mjs, and README commands. `npm run check` passed. Dev server started at http://localhost:3000 and in-app browser verified title/H1/scaffold sections.
tasks.notes: Selected dependency-free Node.js HTTP + ES modules foundation to keep the scaffold runnable without network dependency installation.

tasks.title: Implement domain types and validation rules
tasks.objective: Add typed Cohort15 domain models and validation for event fields, recurrence, quorum, participant cap, expiry default, and link visibility policy.
authority: Implement only behavior specified in the MVP spec
Record any non-blocking product assumptions in code comments or docs
Do not add persistence or UI integration in this task
acceptance_criteria: Event, EventInterest, TokenTransaction, and SocialPost concepts are represented in typed code
Allowed enum values match the spec
Participant cap, recurrence, meeting count, required link, and expiry defaults are validated
Private link visibility policy is represented without leaking links for open events
verification: Run focused domain validation tests
Run type checks if available
tasks.stop_condition: Stop before persistence or UI integration.
tasks.evidence: 2026-05-30 00:18 EDT: Added JSDoc domain model typedefs, spec-aligned enum constants, event and related-object validators, 14-day expiry defaulting, and locked-link viewer serialization policy. `npm run check` passed.
tasks.notes: Private links remain hidden for open events; active-event links are visible only to the creator or interested viewers supplied to the serializer.

tasks.title: Add persistence schema and token ledger primitives
tasks.objective: Create storage for users, events, event interests, token transactions, and social posts, with auditable token balance helpers.
authority: Token balances must be derived from auditable transaction records or kept consistent with them
Do not silently mutate balances without transaction records
Do not implement user-facing create or interest flows in this task
acceptance_criteria: Users, events, interests, token transactions, and social posts can be persisted
Hold, consume, refund, and grant transaction types are supported for MVP
The schema leaves a clear path for a later purchase transaction source without requiring USD payments now
Balance helpers correctly account for held and available tokens
Seed/demo users can receive starting tokens through grant transactions
verification: Run persistence or repository tests for token hold/consume/refund behavior
Run migration/schema validation if available
tasks.evidence: 2026-05-30 00:28 EDT: Added dependency-free in-memory persistence schema/repositories for users, events, interests, token transactions, and social posts; demo seed grants; and token ledger primitives for grant, hold, consume, refund, and derived balances. `npm run check` passed.
tasks.notes: Schema module records a future purchase source path without implementing USD token sales.

tasks.title: Build create cohort flow
tasks.objective: Let an authenticated or demo user create a cohort event by holding 2 tokens and creating an open event with expiresAt equal to createdAt plus 14 days.
authority: Require enough available tokens before event creation
Hide lockedEventLink from public outputs
Do not implement participant interest or expiry automation in this task
acceptance_criteria: Creator with fewer than 2 available tokens cannot create an event
Creator with at least 2 available tokens creates an open event
Creation records a 2-token hold transaction
Event expiry defaults to 14 days after creation
Validation errors are surfaced for invalid event inputs
verification: Run create-flow unit/integration tests
Manually smoke test the create flow if a UI exists
tasks.stop_condition: Stop before participant interest and quorum handling.
tasks.evidence: 2026-05-30 00:33 EDT: Added create cohort service, demo-backed create form route, UI rendering for creation errors/success, and tests for 2-token creator holds, default expiry, insufficient tokens, validation errors, and hidden private links. `npm run check` passed.
tasks.notes: Create flow uses demo users from seed data as the temporary auth path and stops before feed/detail, participant interest, and quorum handling.

tasks.title: Build public event feed and detail visibility
tasks.objective: Show open and active cohort events in a public feed and event detail page while hiding lockedEventLink until quorum unlocks it for authorized users.
write_scope: event visibility serializers or data loaders
authority: Do not expose private online links for open events
Keep filtering/search minimal unless required by existing UI
Do not implement interest mutation behavior in this task
acceptance_criteria: Feed lists public event fields for open and active events
Detail page shows complete public cohort information
Open events show a locked-link placeholder instead of the private link
Active events reveal the private link only according to the chosen authorization policy
verification: Run visibility tests
Manually inspect feed/detail pages if a UI exists
tasks.evidence: 2026-05-30 00:41 EDT: Added event browsing service, public `GET /cohorts` feed, `GET /cohorts/:id` detail route, feed/detail UI rendering, and visibility tests for open/active events, hidden locked links, and authorized active-link viewers. `npm run check` passed.
tasks.notes: Active event links are revealed through the existing conservative authorization policy when `viewerId` identifies the creator or an active interested participant; interest mutation remains T006.

tasks.title: Build show-interest and quorum unlock flow
tasks.objective: Let participants hold 1 token to show interest, prevent duplicate or over-capacity interest, and activate the cohort when active interest count reaches minQuorum.
authority: Participants must have at least 1 available token to show interest
Do not allow duplicate active interest for the same user and event
Do not exceed maxParticipants
Consume held creator and participant tokens only when quorum is met
acceptance_criteria: Showing interest records a 1-token hold and active interest
Duplicate and over-capacity interest attempts are rejected
When active interest count reaches minQuorum, event status becomes active
Quorum consumes held creator and participant tokens
Private event link becomes visible only after activation
verification: Run interest/quorum tests
Manually smoke test interest and unlock if a UI exists
tasks.evidence: 2026-05-30 00:48 EDT: Added show-interest/quorum service, POST interest route, event detail interest UI, consumed-interest link authorization, and tests for 1-token holds, duplicate/cap rejection, quorum activation, token consumption, and unlocked private link rendering. `npm run check` passed. Browser smoke tested create -> detail -> stake interest -> quorum unlock on `http://localhost:3001`.
tasks.notes: Quorum activation consumes creator and participant holds, changes active interests to consumed, and keeps consumed participants authorized to view active private links.

tasks.title: Build expiry and refund processing
tasks.objective: Expire open events past expiresAt that have not met quorum and refund all held creator and participant tokens.
authority: Only open events past expiresAt should be expired
Active events must not be refunded by expiry processing
Refund all held tokens through refund transaction records
acceptance_criteria: Expired events change status to expired
Creator and participant holds are refunded
Interest records become refunded or otherwise clearly non-active
Private event link remains hidden after expiry
verification: Run expiry/refund tests
Run a manual expiry trigger against seeded data if available
tasks.stop_condition: Stop before dashboards and social promotion outbox.
tasks.evidence: 2026-05-30 00:59 EDT: Added expiry processing service, dev/admin POST trigger, and tests for overdue open event expiry, creator/participant refund transactions, refunded interests, active/future event exclusion, and expired cohorts staying non-public. `npm run check` passed; browser smoke confirmed local app still renders.
tasks.notes: Expiry processing is idempotent by only selecting currently open events whose expiresAt is at or before the processing time.

tasks.title: Build social promotion outbox
tasks.objective: Generate a public-safe local social post/outbox record when a cohort is created, containing required public fields and excluding the private link.
write_scope: optional local outbox UI or dev view
authority: Do not add real external social API posting in MVP
Social content must never include lockedEventLink
Status may be pending, posted, or failed according to local/mock behavior
acceptance_criteria: New events create a local social-promotion outbox record
Post text includes event title, topic, target skill level, short description, quorum needed, and public event page link
Post text excludes private event links
Event socialPostStatus is kept consistent with local/mock posting result
verification: Run social promotion tests
Inspect generated post content for a seeded or newly created event
tasks.stop_condition: Stop after local/mock promotion is represented and verifiable.
tasks.evidence: 2026-05-30 00:59 EDT: Added local social promotion service, integrated create cohort with pending social post creation, and tests confirming required public fields and public page URL are included while private links are excluded. `npm run check` passed.
tasks.notes: Real social APIs remain post-MVP. The MVP creates a pending local outbox record on cohort creation and keeps event socialPostStatus consistent with the post status.

tasks.title: Build creator and participant dashboards
tasks.objective: Provide dashboard views for creators and participants showing relevant cohorts, statuses, token holds, and unlocked links where allowed.
authority: Keep dashboards focused on MVP status and token visibility
Do not add analytics beyond what is needed to understand cohort state
Respect private link visibility rules
acceptance_criteria: Creator dashboard lists owned cohorts and statuses
Creator dashboard shows relevant token hold/consume/refund state
Participant dashboard lists cohorts where the user showed interest
Unlocked links appear only when the event is active and the user is authorized
verification: Run dashboard tests
Manually inspect dashboards with seeded creator and participant data if a UI exists
tasks.stop_condition: Stop before broad polish or analytics.
tasks.evidence: 2026-05-30 00:59 EDT: Added dashboard data service, creator and participant dashboard routes/UI, and tests for owned/interested cohorts, token held/consume/refund summaries, and authorized unlocked links. `npm run check` passed; browser smoke checked `/dashboard/creator` and `/dashboard/participant`.
tasks.notes: Dashboards use demo query-param user selection until regular auth is specified, matching existing create/detail MVP assumptions.

tasks.title: Add MVP verification pass and docs
tasks.objective: Add final coverage and documentation for the core success path, expiry path, token accounting, and local operation.
authority: May fix small defects discovered while verifying existing MVP behavior
Do not add new product feature scope
Document unresolved assumptions rather than silently broadening scope
acceptance_criteria: Core success path is covered by automated tests or documented manual verification
Expiry/refund path is covered by automated tests or documented manual verification
Token ledger behavior is covered by tests
README documents setup, run, test, seed/demo data, MVP boundary, post-MVP USD token packages, and known assumptions
verification: Run the full documented verification suite
Manually exercise the MVP flow if UI automation is not available
tasks.stop_condition: Stop once the MVP can be handed to user testing.
tasks.evidence: 2026-05-30 07:42 EDT: Added MVP verification coverage for the full create/promote/quorum/dashboard success path and the create/interest/expiry/refund path; expanded README with demo data, MVP flow, local expiry, social outbox, post-MVP token package assumptions, and known assumptions. `npm run check` passed with 36 tests. Dev server started at `http://localhost:3000`; in-app browser smoke verified create page and successful cohort creation without exposing the private link in the success state.
tasks.notes: Manual browser feed/unlock follow-up was stopped by the browser URL policy after creation; the new automated verification test covers feed, detail, quorum unlock, dashboards, and expiry/refund end to end.

tasks.title: Add durable persistence adapter
tasks.objective: Replace or augment the in-memory repositories with a durable local persistence adapter while preserving the existing repository contracts and token ledger semantics.
authority: May introduce a durable local database dependency if needed
Must keep token balances derived from transaction records
Must keep existing service-level behavior compatible with current tests
Do not implement auth, payment, or social API behavior in this task
acceptance_criteria: Users, events, interests, token transactions, and social posts persist across app restarts when durable mode is enabled
Existing in-memory test setup remains available or tests are updated to use an isolated durable store
Token hold, consume, refund, grant, and future purchase transaction semantics remain auditable
README documents how local persistence is configured and reset
verification: Run persistence tests covering restart/reload behavior
Run the full documented verification suite
tasks.stop_condition: Stop once durable persistence is available behind the repository boundary and documented.
tasks.notes: Durable persistence is the first post-MVP hardening task because later auth and purchases need stable user and ledger records.

tasks.title: Replace demo user selection with regular auth boundary
tasks.objective: Add a regular authentication boundary so create, interest, dashboard, and private-link authorization use the signed-in user instead of demo query parameters or default users.
authority: May choose the simplest local auth/session approach that fits the dependency-free app unless the user specifies a provider
Must remove user-controlled query/default-user paths from protected mutations and dashboards
Must preserve conservative private-link authorization
Do not implement OAuth provider integrations unless explicitly chosen
acceptance_criteria: Unauthenticated users cannot create cohorts, show interest, or view dashboards
Authenticated users can create cohorts and show interest using their own user id
Creator and participant dashboards are scoped to the signed-in user
Private links remain visible only to authorized signed-in creators or committed participants
Demo users remain available only through an explicit local development path if retained
verification: Run auth route and service integration tests
Run the full documented verification suite
tasks.stop_condition: Stop once user identity is resolved through the auth boundary for all protected MVP flows.
tasks.notes: Provider choice is still open; this task should document any local/session assumption instead of pretending it is production identity.

tasks.title: Add token purchase package flow
tasks.objective: Add the first token purchase flow for the documented post-MVP packages, recording purchase transactions without breaking grant or hold accounting.
write_scope: token package constants or service modules
token transaction schema fields if needed
authority: Use initial packages of $6 for 6 tokens and $12 for 14 tokens
May implement a local/mock payment confirmation unless a real payment provider is specified
Must record successful purchases as auditable purchase token transactions
Do not store real card data or implement unrequested payment-provider credentials
acceptance_criteria: Authenticated users can select one of the two documented token packages
Successful purchase creates a purchase transaction and updates derived available balance
Cancelled or failed local/mock purchases do not grant tokens
Dashboards or token summary surfaces reflect purchased token balance
README clearly labels whether payment is local/mock or provider-backed
verification: Run purchase flow tests
Run token ledger tests
Run the full documented verification suite
tasks.stop_condition: Stop once package purchase accounting works and payment-provider limitations are documented.
tasks.notes: A real provider is not specified, so local/mock purchase mode is acceptable if clearly documented.

tasks.title: Publish social outbox to configured external channels
tasks.objective: Turn the local social-promotion outbox into a publishable channel adapter flow that can post public-safe cohort content to configured official channels.
authority: Must keep private event links out of all generated and published social content
May implement dry-run/mock adapters if official channels or credentials are absent
Must record posted or failed status per SocialPost
Do not hard-code secrets or require credentials for normal test runs
acceptance_criteria: Pending social posts can be processed through a configured adapter boundary
Successful publishes record platform post URLs when available and mark posts posted
Failed publishes capture failure state without losing the outbox record
Dry-run or mock mode remains available for local development and tests
Tests prove private links are never sent to adapters
verification: Run social publishing tests with a mock adapter
Run the full documented verification suite
tasks.stop_condition: Stop once external-channel publishing is adapter-backed, testable, and safely configurable.
tasks.notes: Official channels and credentials are still unknown; adapter boundaries and dry-run behavior should come before real API credentials.

tasks.title: Add cohort completion and cancellation lifecycle handling
tasks.objective: Implement bounded creator/admin lifecycle controls for cancelling open cohorts and completing active cohorts while preserving token accounting and link visibility rules.
authority: May support cancelled and completed statuses already defined in the domain
Cancelling open cohorts must refund held creator and participant tokens through refund transactions
Completing active cohorts must not refund already consumed tokens
Do not add broad moderation tooling in this task
acceptance_criteria: Authorized creators or admins can cancel open cohorts
Cancellation refunds held creator and participant tokens and removes the cohort from public discovery
Authorized creators or admins can mark active cohorts completed
Completed cohorts keep historical dashboard visibility without exposing links to unauthorized users
Unauthorized lifecycle actions are rejected
verification: Run lifecycle service and route tests
Run the full documented verification suite
tasks.stop_condition: Stop once cancelled and completed lifecycle paths are implemented with tests and documented behavior.
tasks.notes: This uses existing domain statuses that were modeled but not implemented in the MVP.
